topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification that made my stomach clench. A profile picture flashed onto the screen—the same sneering face I hadn't seen in fifteen years. It was Mark. My childhood tormentor. A friend request. A cold wave washed over me, leaving a clammy feeling on my skin. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. Why now? The air in my small apartment seemed to thicken, pressing in on me. I stared at the little blue 'Accept' button, a knot tightening in my chest with every second that ticked by. I quickly swiped the notification away, shoving the phone face down on the worn wooden table, wishing the floor would just swallow me whole.

[story 2]

The chime of the notification ripped through the quiet evening. I snatched my phone up, my breath catching sharply in my throat. It was him. Mark. The memory of his sharp words and the way he used to make me feel small flooded back, and a sudden heat rushed up my neck. My hands started to shake so badly I had to grip the edge of the desk to steady them. What did this mean? I reread the request three times, my eyes scanning the screen as if searching for an escape route. A hollow ache settled deep in my core. I felt a desperate urge to throw the device across the room, to shatter the glass and silence the intrusion.

[story 3]

When I saw the notification, a strange paralysis seized me. It was Mark. The name alone was enough to send a jolt of icy fear through my system. I leaned back in my chair, my spine stiffening as if bracing for impact. My vision blurred for a second, and I blinked hard to clear the sudden fuzziness. Every muscle in my body felt coiled tight, ready to bolt, but my feet remained rooted to the floor. I kept staring at the screen, unable to move, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic drumming of my pulse against my ribs. I slowly, painstakingly, navigated to the settings, my movements sluggish and heavy.
